perturbation.original_text: Unless sooner terminated by mutual written Agreement of the Parties hereto, this Agreement and the obligations hereunder shall terminate one (1) year from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless sooner terminated by mutual written Agreement of the Parties hereto, this Agreement and the obligations hereunder shall terminate two (2) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the termination date of the agreement from one year to two years. This contradicts the initial intention and any implied timelines in other sections that might rely on the original one-year term. For example, discussions or obligations expected to be completed within a year are now unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of each Party's providing Confidential Information, the Parties agree as follows: 1 a. The Receiving Party shall hold confidential and not disclose to any Person, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, all Confidential Information and any information about the Proposed Transaction, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives; provided, however, that the Receiving Party may disclose such Confidential Information to its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction; b. The Receiving Party shall cause all its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement and shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives; and c. The Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request. In addition to the foregoing, the Receiving Party will not use the Confidential Information (a) in any way detrimental to the Disclosing Party’s shareholders or (b) for any purpose other than in connection with the Proposed Transaction between the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a one-year termination date, while the changed text extends it to two years. This directly contradicts the expectations and implied timelines within the contradicted section concerning Confidential Information obligations. The contradicted text stipulates returning or destroying confidential information within 30 days of a written request. The extension of the agreement to two years now introduces uncertainty and a direct contradiction about how long those obligations should exist for and when the parties can expect the confidential information to be returned/destroyed, constituting a strong in-text contradiction as described by the Inconsistencies definition. If the original text was not changed, there would be a direct contradiction

perturbation.original_text: “ Representatives ” shall mean as to any Person, its directors, officers, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys and accountants).
perturbation.changed_text: “ Representatives ” shall mean as to any Person, its directors, officers, employees, and agents. Advisors, including financial advisors, attorneys, and accountants, are explicitly excluded from the definition of 'Representatives' for the purpose of data access and liability under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by excluding advisors from the definition of 'Representatives'. This contradicts the original definition, which included advisors, and creates uncertainty about whether advisors are subject to the confidentiality obligations and liabilities outlined in the agreement. Specifically, it clashes with Section 2, which holds Representatives responsible for breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of each Party's providing Confidential Information, the Parties agree as follows: 1 a. The Receiving Party shall hold confidential and not disclose to any Person, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, all Confidential Information and any information about the Proposed Transaction, or the terms or conditions or any other facts relating thereto, including, without limitation, the fact that discussions are taking place with respect thereto or the status thereof, or the fact that Confidential Information has been made available to the Receiving Party or its Representatives; provided, however, that the Receiving Party may disclose such Confidential Information to its Representatives who are actively and directly participating in its evaluation of the Proposed Transaction or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating the Proposed Transaction; b. The Receiving Party shall cause all its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement and shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives; and c. The Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request. In addition to the foregoing, the Receiving Party will not use the Confidential Information (a) in any way detrimental to the Disclosing Party’s shareholders or (b) for any purpose other than in connection with the Proposed Transaction between the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes advisors from the definition of 'Representatives', while the contradicted text in Section 1b states that the Receiving Party is responsible for any breach of the agreement by its 'Representatives'. Since advisors are no longer considered 'Representatives' under the modified definition, the Receiving Party would not be responsible for breaches committed by advisors, which contradicts the original intent and creates a direct conflict within the document, causing an inconsistency as defined above.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information " shall mean all confidential or proprietary written, recorded, electronic or oral information or data (including without limitation research, developmental, engineering, manufacturing, technical, marketing, sales, financial, operating, performance, cost, business and process information or data, trade secrets, discoveries, ideas, designs, data, source code, object code, processes, computer programs, developments, flow diagrams, know-how, and computer programming and other software and software techniques) provided (whether such confidentiality or proprietary status is indicated orally or in writing, whether or not the specific words "confidential" or "proprietary" are used) to a Party (the “Receiving Party”) by the other Party (the “Disclosing Party”) in the course of the exchange of such information or data between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information " shall mean all confidential or proprietary written, recorded, electronic or oral information or data (excluding customer lists, pricing strategies, and marketing plans) provided (whether such confidentiality or proprietary status is indicated orally or in writing, whether or not the specific words "confidential" or "proprietary" are used) to a Party (the “Receiving Party”) by the other Party (the “Disclosing Party”) in the course of the exchange of such information or data between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction by explicitly excluding certain types of information (customer lists, pricing strategies, and marketing plans) from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The original definition was broad and all-encompassing. This contradicts the implicit understanding that all exchanged information related to the potential transaction would be kept confidential, leading to disputes if these excluded categories are later disclosed. This now creates a conflict in Section 3, where exceptions to confidentiality are defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations imposed by Section 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall cease to apply, to any Confidential Information if or when, but only to the extent that, such Confidential Information: a. was known to the Receiving Party prior to the receipt of the Confidential Information; or b. was, or becomes through no breach of the Receiving Party's obligations hereunder, known to the public; or c. becomes known to the Receiving Party from sources other than the Disclosing Party under circumstances not involving any breach of any confidentiality obligation; or d. is independently developed by the Receiving Party, as evidenced by the written records thereof. It shall not be a breach of the confidentiality obligations hereof for a Receiving Party to disclose Confidential Information where, but only to the extent that, such disclosure is required by law or applicable legal process, provided in such case the Receiving Party shall (i) give the earliest notice possible to the Disclosing Party that such disclosure is or may be required and (ii) reasonably cooperate in protecting such confidential or proprietary nature of the Confidential Information which must so be disclosed. 2
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text defines 'Confidential Information' in a broad, all-encompassing manner. The changed text explicitly excludes 'customer lists, pricing strategies, and marketing plans' from this definition. This contradicts Section 3, which defines exceptions to confidentiality, because now, certain information is categorically excluded from being considered confidential at all, regardless of whether those exceptions in Section 3 apply. The original text did not exclude these items, thereby requiring section 3 exceptions to apply. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the Parties make no representation or warranties, express or implied, of any kind to the other Party with respect to the Confidential Information, including without limitation with respect to the accuracy or completeness thereof. Any representations or warranties shall be made thereby, if at all, only in definitive written agreements that may be entered into hereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Parties make no representation or warranties, express or implied, of any kind to the other Party with respect to the Confidential Information, except that the Disclosing Party warrants that it has the legal right to disclose said Confidential Information. Any further representations or warranties shall be made thereby, if at all, only in definitive written agreements that may be entered into hereafter.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a specific warranty that the Disclosing Party has the legal right to disclose the Confidential Information. This contradicts the original statement that 'no representation or warranties, express or implied, of any kind' are made. This warranty creates potential liability and conflicts with the general disclaimer. It now has a direct effect in Section 10, which discuss Remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to either Party, each Party shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement and the Receiving Party shall not plead in defense thereto that there would be an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'no representation or warranties, express or implied, of any kind' are made regarding Confidential Information. The changed text introduces an exception by warranting that the Disclosing Party has the legal right to disclose said Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the earlier statement of 'no warranties', creating an inconsistency. This contradiction directly affects Section 10, which discusses remedies, as the warranty now creates potential liability and conflicts with the general disclaimer. Thus, there is a direct and impactful contradiction.
